data.title: Hallenstein Glasson CEO lifts stake with on-market share purchase
data.description:

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited ( (NZ:HLG) ) has shared an update.

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings chief executive James McLauchlan has increased his personal stake in the retailer, disclosing an on-market purchase of 3,500 ordinary shares on 17 April 2026. The transaction lifted his beneficial holding, held together with Lily Carlyon through JBWere (NZ) Nominees, from 5,000 to 8,500 shares at a total cost of $34,049.65.

The acquisition, made outside a closed trading period and with no use of derivatives, marginally strengthens insider alignment with shareholders at the NZX-listed clothing group. While small in scale relative to the company’s overall register, the move signals executive confidence in Hallenstein Glasson’s prospects and will be closely noted by investors tracking management’s trading activity.

More about Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited is a New Zealand-listed apparel retailer operating under the Hallensteins and Glassons brands. The company focuses on fashion clothing and accessories, targeting mainstream consumers across New Zealand and Australia through its store network and online channels.

Average Trading Volume: 25,863

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: N$598.9M

Argosy Minerals ( (AU:AGY) ) has provided an update.

Argosy Minerals has called its Annual General Meeting of shareholders for 22 May 2026 at 1:30 p.m. WST in Perth, Western Australia, and will make the Notice of Meeting available digitally on its website rather than mailing physical copies. Shareholders who have not opted for email communications will receive personalised proxy forms by post and are encouraged to lodge their proxy votes by 20 May 2026, underscoring the company’s ongoing shift to electronic dissemination of corporate information and formalising governance arrangements for the upcoming meeting.

More about Argosy Minerals

Argosy Minerals Limited is an Australian-listed resources company focused on mineral exploration and development, trading on the ASX under the ticker AGY. The company operates within the mining sector, though this notice does not specify its primary commodities or project locations, and is headquartered in Perth, Western Australia.

data.title: Reach Resources Director Sees 600,000 Performance Rights Expire at No Value
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Reach Resources Limited ( (AU:RR1) ) has issued an update.

Reach Resources Limited has disclosed a change in director Robert Downey’s indirect interests following the expiry of certain performance-based securities held via his spouse, Stella Emily Downey. A total of 600,000 non-executive director performance rights in Tranche B lapsed at no value after the relevant performance milestones were not met.

Following the expiry, Downey’s indirect interests continue to comprise fully paid ordinary shares, listed options, and substantial unlisted options across two tranches. The update, lodged under ASX disclosure rules, slightly reduces his performance-based exposure but leaves his overall equity and option alignment with shareholders largely intact.

More about Reach Resources Limited

Reach Resources Limited is an Australian company listed on the ASX, operating in the resources sector. The company is involved in the exploration and development of mineral assets, providing investors exposure to commodities-focused projects within the Australian market.

Stanmore Resources Ltd ( (AU:SMR) ) has issued an announcement.

Stanmore Resources has released its 2025 Annual Report, outlining the performance of its metallurgical coal operations in Queensland’s Bowen Basin and its portfolio of undeveloped coal projects and tenements. The report includes the chair’s and CEO’s commentary, a detailed directors’ report, audited financial statements and sustainability reporting, offering investors and stakeholders a consolidated view of the company’s governance, financial health and long-term development focus.

The inclusion of a dedicated sustainability report and independent assurance reflects growing emphasis on environmental and social performance within the coal sector. By highlighting both operating mines and future development opportunities, Stanmore signals its intention to optimise existing assets while progressing growth projects, a strategy that may influence its competitive positioning in metallurgical coal markets and its appeal to capital providers.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SMR) stock is a Buy with a A$3.65 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Stanmore Resources Ltd stock, see the AU:SMR Stock Forecast page.

More about Stanmore Resources Ltd

Stanmore Resources Limited is an Australian coal producer listed on the ASX, operating the South Walker Creek, Poitrel and Isaac Plains Complex metallurgical coal mines in Queensland’s Bowen Basin. The company also holds undeveloped projects such as Isaac Downs Extension, Eagle Downs, Lancewood and Isaac Plains underground, along with additional prospective coal tenements in the Bowen and Surat basins.

China LNG Group ( (HK:0931) ) has provided an update.

China HK Power Smart Energy Group Limited has requested a halt in trading of its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, effective from 9:00 a.m. on 20 April 2026, as it prepares to release an announcement containing insider information. The trading suspension signals that potentially price-sensitive developments are pending, and investors will be closely watching the forthcoming disclosure for its impact on the company’s operations, governance and market valuation.

The board of the company, chaired by Dr. Kan Che Kin, Billy Albert and comprising a mix of executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, formally approved the request for the halt. The breadth of board representation underscores that the decision reflects a coordinated governance response to material information, aiming to ensure fair and orderly trading when the company’s shares resume on the Hong Kong market.

More about China LNG Group

China HK Power Smart Energy Group Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the main board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 931. The group operates in the power and smart energy sector, focusing on energy-related businesses within Greater China and leveraging its public listing to access capital markets and support its development strategy.

Arena REIT ( (AU:ARF) ) just unveiled an update.

Arena REIT has updated its notification of a quarterly distribution on its fully paid ordinary stapled securities for the period ended 31 March 2026. The update, dated 20 April 2026, clarifies that the change from the prior 24 March announcement is to provide pricing details for the distribution reinvestment plan attached to this payment.

The distribution relates to the quarter ended 31 March 2026, with an ex‑date of 30 March and a record date of 31 March for eligible securityholders. Payment will be made on the scheduled dividend distribution date, and the additional DRP pricing information gives investors clearer options for receiving their entitlement in cash or additional securities.

Ramsay Health Care ( (AU:RHC) ) has provided an announcement.

Ramsay Health Care has declared a six‑month distribution on its RHCPA transferable preference securities, set at AUD 3.3608 per security. The distribution will trade ex‑distribution on 29 September 2026, with a record date of 30 September and payment scheduled for 20 October, signalling ongoing servicing of its hybrid capital instruments for investors.

The payment relates to the period ending 19 October 2026 and is classified as an ordinary distribution under the RHCPA terms. By maintaining regular payouts on these perpetual subordinated redeemable securities, Ramsay underscores the stability of its capital structure and provides income continuity to holders of its hybrid securities.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RHC) stock is a Hold with a A$39.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ramsay Health Care stock, see the AU:RHC Stock Forecast page.

data.description:

The latest update is out from Invion ( (AU:IVX) ).

Invion Limited has disclosed changes to the relevant interests of director Chiat Thian Chew in the company’s securities, as required under ASX listing rules. The notice details both his direct holdings in ordinary shares and unlisted options and his indirect interests through associated entities and individuals.

Following the change dated 20 April 2026, Chew’s indirect interests were expanded to include additional ordinary shares held via RMWC Pty Ltd and associate Mr Honsue Cho, although no consideration was paid. The update clarifies that Chew, as Managing Partner of Polar Ventures Limited, is taken to be an associate of these parties, highlighting a consolidation and formal recognition of his associated holdings rather than a cash-funded market transaction.

More about Invion

Invion Limited is an Australian-listed company, though the release does not specify its industry, products, or services. The filing focuses on changes in a director’s securities holdings and associated interests rather than operational or market activities.
